title: 中考六选五 5: Learning in the Age of Short Videos
passage:
Short videos have become part of many students’ daily lives. They are fast, interesting and easy to watch. A student can learn a cooking tip, a history fact or an English phrase in less than a minute. 1. ______

One advantage of short videos is that they can make learning less frightening. A difficult topic may become easier to enter through a short explanation. 2. ______ This can be useful, especially when students are first introduced to a new idea.

However, short videos often simplify knowledge. To fit into a short time, they may leave out background, evidence or exceptions. 3. ______ Students may think they understand a topic, but actually only know a small piece of it.

Another issue is attention. Short videos train people to expect quick rewards. After watching many of them, students may find textbooks or long articles boring. 4. ______ This is dangerous because many important ideas require slow thinking.

The better approach is to use short videos as a starting point, not the whole learning process. Students can use them to discover interest or get a simple overview. 5. ______ Real understanding still needs reading, practice, questioning and time.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: But they should continue with deeper study after that.
The problem is that quick learning may also create shallow understanding.
In this way, students may become willing to learn more.
As a result, the information can seem clearer than it really is.
Their patience for deeper learning may gradually become weaker.
Short videos are usually more useful than books for serious study.
